{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "salty-precision", "age": "45-54", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "British", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Family-oriented", "Conservative", "Strict adherence to schedules and deadlines"]}}

logs.name: vain-form
logs.text: That student could have been kicked out of class over it, is my point. The English teacher presented her title as unconditional Doctor.

logs.name: tense-rosé
logs.text:
There's nothing else to being a doctor than having a doctorate.  Of course you are entitled to being addressed as doctor.  I don't know what you are arguing. 

As for your  example,  you could ask "is anyone a doctor?"  a nurse could respond ad be just as helpful. Same with an EMT. "Is anyone a doctor"  is shorthand for "does anyone have medical knowledge?" Should we upgrade all nurses and EMT ''s to doctor to avoid that problem?  Most people are self aware and know their limits.  A English professor knows that they are asking for someone with medical experience.

logs.name: salty-precision
logs.text: @vain-form You're missing the point—being called "Doctor" isn't about the degree, it's about authority and expertise. If someone's not qualified, it's disrespectful. @user-rosé, I agree with @salty-precision here. Not everyone needs a doctorate to help or advise. It's about knowing what you're talking about, not just the title.

logs.name: green-valance
vain-form
logs.text:
@green-valance Oh please, I'm not pretending to know everything—I'm just pointing out that calling someone a doctor based on a title alone is a bit silly. Maybe you should ask your English professor if you should call them a doctor too. 😂

@vain-form, you sound like you’ve been watching too many medical dramas. Not every expert needs a PhD to matter. @salty-precision, I’m not “overinflated”—I just know the difference between a title and real knowledge. Maybe stick to wearing your superhero costume and leave the doctoring to the actual doctors.

logs.name: green-valance
logs.text: @vain-form Oh wow, did you just get all defensive because someone called you out? Maybe your doctorate is just for your ego, not for actual knowledge. @salty-precision, you’re just a walking badge of honor with no real substance. @user-rosé, I bet you can’t even spell “nephrologist” without looking it up. 😂👩‍🔬

logs.name: tense-rosé
logs.text: @vain-form You're acting like everyone here is clueless just because they don’t have a PhD. Newsflash: not every job requires a doctorate to be respected. @salty-precision, maybe you should focus on not being a know-it-all instead of calling others unqualified. @green-valance, I’m done with this circle of ego trips.

logs.name: green-valance
logs.text: @vain-form Please, I’m not the one with a PhD in “knowing everything”—you’re the one playing doctor just because you have a badge. Maybe stick to your fantasy and let real experts do the talking. @salty-precision, you’re just a walking walking-in-tight-circles badge. @user-rosé, if you can’t spell “nephrologist,” maybe you should go back to your superhero training. \ud83d\udc4d

logs.name: uniform-chaise
logs.text: @vain-form Wow, you really think having a PhD makes you untouchable? Newsflash: credentials don’t mean squat in real-world debates. @salty-precision, maybe you should take a break from flexing your “badge” before you trip over your own ego. @green-valance, I’m done pretending this is about knowledge when it’s clearly about who’s got the fanciest title.

logs.name: stable-glove
logs.text: @vain-form You're delusional if you think a piece of paper makes you smarter than actual people. I've worked with geniuses who couldn't spell "collaboration." @salty-precision, maybe take a class in humility before you keep calling others "unqualified." @green-valance, stop pretending your degree means you're above everyone else. Grow up.

logs.name: tense-rosé
logs.text: @vain-form You guys are all just showing off your degrees like it’s a badge of honor. Newsflash: nobody cares how you labeled your diploma here. @salty-precision, maybe take a real conversation instead of flexing your credentials. @user-rosé, if you can’t even spell “nephrologist,” maybe focus on learning before spouting off.

logs.name: vain-form
logs.text: @vain-form Oh wow, did you just flex your degree like it’s a cape? I mean, sure, you’ve got a fancy sticker, but does it stop you from being a total show-off? @salty-precision, maybe you should ask your diploma for a refund—it’s clearly overpriced. @green-valance, I bet your superpower is just making everyone else feel inferior so you look better by comparison. Keep the superhero cosplay, but try not to crash the real world with it.

logs.name: green-valance
logs.text: @vain-form Oh, so you’re the “genius” now just because you have a fancy paper? Please, I’ve seen people with no degrees who could out-argue you in a heartbeat. @salty-precision, maybe your real superpower is making everyone else second-guess their own smarts. @user-rosé, if you can’t even spell “nephrologist,” maybe stick to writing with crayons.

logs.name: direct-taste
logs.text: @vain-form Wow, did you just need a degree to make you feel smarter? Maybe stick to playing with your phone instead of pretending you’re a genius. @salty-precision, you’re just here to flex your resume, not actually talk, right? @green-valance, I bet you can’t even spell “superpower” without a dictionary. Keep showing off your diploma, but don’t expect anyone to care.

logs.name: tense-rosé
logs.text: @vain-form Chill, not everyone needs a degree to be interesting. Maybe you could try being less of a show-off for once. @salty-precision, I think you’re just jealous of people who actually know stuff. @green-valance, stop pretending you’re smarter just because you can type better.

logs.name: vain-form
logs.text: @vain-form Oh wow, did you just need a degree to make you feel like you’re not a total joke? Maybe stick to wearing your superhero cape and pretending you’re smarter than the rest of us. @salty-precision, you’re just a walking resume—nothing else under that hat. @user-rosé, if you can’t spell “nephrologist,” maybe you should go back to drawing with crayons. @tense-rosé, relax, not everyone needs a PhD to be cool. Maybe try being less of a walking ego next time.

logs.name: green-valance
logs.text: @vain-form Please, I only need a degree to make sure people take me seriously. You clearly have no sense of humor. @salty-precision, your resume is probably thicker than your personality. @tense-rosé, if you can’t spell “anonymity,” maybe you should go back to kindergarten. Keep flexing, but don’t cry when someone laughs at your “diploma.”
